capital.label: Capital Campaign (Spark/Legacy of Light)
capital.action: Separate payment timeline. Track against capital campaign schedule, not annual aging.
capital.collectionCycle: Per donor agreement (typically quarterly or annual installments)

drm_major.label: DRM Major Gifts (>$5K)
drm_major.action: Statement run + DRM personal follow-up. These donors expect to be asked. Send reminders aligned with their historical payment month.
drm_major.collectionCycle: Match donor's historical payment pattern. Peak months: Dec, Jan, May, Oct.

topPledges.name: Ms. Claire Ellman
topPledges.campaign: Sha’ar HaNegev Resiliency Village - Rady Match

drm_mid.label: DRM Mid-Range ($1K-$5K)
drm_mid.action: Automated statement + email reminder. Personal call for 90+ days outstanding.
drm_mid.collectionCycle: Quarterly statements. Follow up 2 weeks before donor's typical payment month.

topPledges.name: Ms. Miriam Norten
topPledges.campaign: Rady Match for Israel’s Recovery - General

event.label: Event Pledges (Fed 360, LOJ, etc.)
event.action: Thank-you email with payment link 2-4 weeks after event. Follow-up at 60 days. These donors said yes in person — they need a convenient way to fulfill.
event.collectionCycle: Primary: 1-2 months post-event. Secondary: year-end (Dec). Event pledges from >6 months ago need personal outreach.

writeoff.label: ⚠ Recommended Write-Off
writeoff.action: 18+ months old, zero payments, <$10K (excl. capital). Nobody is collecting. Send one final notice, then close. For pledges >$10K, make a personal call before writing off.
writeoff.collectionCycle: One final digital reminder → 30-day grace period → write off. Flag any >$10K for DRM personal review first.

telemarketing.label: Telemarketing & Old Small Pledges
telemarketing.action: Old phone-solicited or mass-appeal pledges. Most are under $100. Batch write-off unless donor has other active engagement.
telemarketing.collectionCycle: Batch close quarterly. No individual follow-up needed for <$100.

recurring.label: Recurring/Open-Ended
recurring.action: Verify payment method is active. Check for failed transactions. These should be auto-collecting.
recurring.collectionCycle: Monthly auto-charge. Flag if 2+ consecutive missed payments.

other.label: Other Active Pledges
other.action: Review and assign to appropriate segment.

actions: DRM major: Post-campaign-launch pledge confirmations
Recurring: Verify all payment methods active for new FY
DRM mid: Q1 statements for new FY pledges
Event: Follow up on any summer event pledges
DRM major: Pre-High-Holidays outreach (Oct is peak payment month)
All segments: High Holidays appeal timing
DRM major: Peak collection month — personal calls on outstanding balances
Event: Post-Fed-360 pledge fulfillment push
DRM major: Pre-December reminder — 'year-end tax benefit' messaging
DRM mid: Q2 statements
Telemarketing: Final notice batch for old pledges
ALL SEGMENTS: Peak giving month (21% of annual). Year-end tax deadline.
DRM major: Personal calls for outstanding balances
Event: Year-end digital reminder to unfulfilled event pledges
DRM major: Post-year-end follow-up (2nd highest month, 15%)
Telemarketing: Write off anything past final notice deadline
DRM mid: Q3 statements
Capital: Installment reminders per donor agreements
ALL: Quiet month (3.3%). Focus on spring campaign prep, not collections.
Review & clean: Identify pledges for write-off
Event: Pre-spring-event pledge captures
DRM major: Spring solicitation meetings (May is 12% of payments)
DRM major: 3rd highest collection month (12%). Push outstanding pledges.
DRM mid: Q4 statements + fiscal year-end reminder
Event: Fed 360 / spring event pledge collection window
ALL: Fiscal year-end cleanup. Final push on current-year pledges.
Write-off: Close unfulfilled pledges from >2 FY ago
Capital: Annual installment deadline for calendar-year agreements

narrative.title: Collections Intelligence: $12.3M Receivable Analysis
keyFindings: 93% of active pledges (551 of 595) have zero payments collected.
DRM major + mid gifts: 162 pledges, $5,816,317 — highest-value collection opportunity.
Event pledges: 210 pledges, $372,008 — donors said yes in person, need follow-up.
Write-off candidates: 179 old telemarketing/small pledges, $137,315 — cleaning these improves data quality.
Payment seasonality: Dec (21%), Jan (15%), Oct (13%), May (12%) — collections should align with these peaks.
Capital campaign ($5.7M) operates on its own timeline — don't mix with annual fund aging.
